response: The coach who was replaced in 1996 as the longest tenured active coach in both the NBA and all U.S. major sports leagues was **Marvin Lewis**. He was the head coach of the Cincinnati Bengals and had been the longest tenured head coach in Bengals' history. In 1996, he was replaced by **Gregg Popovich**, who took over as the head coach of the San Antonio Spurs. Popovich became the longest tenured active coach in both the NBA and all U.S. major sports leagues following Lewis's tenure.
